title: Toy generation for unequal binning RooDataHist

posts.name: Xuli Yan
posts.text:
Hello experts,
Recently I have been trying to generate toys for unequal binning RooDataHists using the combine tool method "combine -M GenerateOnly ", but somehow when plotting the toys generated, I found something weird, as shown in the plot (blue points: data from exp function, blue curve, exp function, red points, toy generated with combine tool):

toy_52396×1772 55.4 KB

Do you know if this is expected?
If you would need some additional information, I do have a minimum working example, using a simple exponential function to generate the toy and plot it.
generate_debug.zip (79.3 KB)
Thanks a lot!
Best regards,
Sebastian
posts.cooked_html:

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
You have a lot of bins at 0 for the red (induced somehow by the binning).
The dataset seems to be correct. Probably, you will have to clean up the plotting.
Below the comparison of data (blue) and a toy (red).
toy_1_0.png2396×1772 47.4 KB
On Wed, May 11, 2022 at 1:35 PM Xuli Yan via CMS talk <noreply@cms-talk.web.cern.ch> wrote:

Xuli Yan xuyanMay 11

Hello experts,
Recently I have been trying to generate toys for unequal binning RooDataHists using the combine tool method "combine -M GenerateOnly ", but somehow when plotting the toys generated, I found something weird, as shown in the plot (blue points: data from exp function, blue curve, exp function, red points, toy generated with combine tool):

Do you know if this is expected?
If you would need some additional information, I do have a minimum working example, using a simple exponential function to generate the toy and plot it.
Generation_debug.zip (131.6 KB)
Thanks a lot!
Best regards,
Sebastian

posts.name: Xuli Yan
posts.text:
Hi Andrea,
Ah okay! Indeed it seems we need to set the binning when plotting explicitly.
Thanks a lot for your kind help!
Best regards,
Sebastian
posts.cooked_html:
